skill.name: add-uint-support
skill.description: Add unsigned integer (uint) type support to PyTorch operators by updating AT_DISPATCH macros. Use when adding support for uint16, uint32, uint64 types to operators, kernels, or when user mentions enabling unsigned types, barebones unsigned types, or uint support.
skill.summary: Guide PyTorch operator updates for uint16, uint32, and uint64 dispatch support.
skill.author: pytorch

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged markdown backticks, uint-related text, and C++ example function names as risky patterns. Manual review found these are false positives in documentation and C++ macro examples, with no executable scripts, network access, credential access, prompt injection, or exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown and C++ Examples Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command detections point to fenced markdown and inline C++ dispatch macro examples. The skill contains instructional text for editing PyTorch C++ macros and does not define Ruby backtick execution or shell command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside markdown code fences or inline macro references. No shell interpreter, Ruby execution context, script file, or command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Matched Non-Cryptographic Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in the skill description and dispatch type reference. The text discusses unsigned integer type groups and does not describe hashing, encryption, or cryptographic algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line context is about PyTorch unsigned integer support and AT_DISPATCH type groups. There is no cryptographic API, hash function, or security-sensitive algorithm use.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern Matched Example Function Names
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detections occur in C++ example kernel function names. The examples do not collect host data, run discovery commands, or inspect system configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are function declarations in a C++ documentation example. The surrounding lines show tensor iterator dispatch examples, not host reconnaissance behavior.

content.user_title: Add PyTorch Unsigned Integer Dispatch Support
content.value_statement: PyTorch operators may miss uint16, uint32, and uint64 coverage in dispatch macros. This skill guides safe AT_DISPATCH_V2 updates so unsigned integer support is added consistently.
actual_capabilities: Identifies PyTorch dispatch macros that need uint16, uint32, and uint64 support.
Explains when to add AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES explicitly.
Explains when to replace AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES with AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES_V2.
Guides conversion planning when an operator still uses older AT_DISPATCH macros.
Checks multiple dispatch sites for consistent unsigned integer coverage.
Highlights edge cases where unsigned integer support may not be semantically valid.
limitations: It does not prove that an operator is mathematically valid for unsigned types.
It does not run PyTorch tests or compile kernels by itself.
It focuses on AT_DISPATCH macro updates, not full kernel implementation design.
It depends on the user or agent to inspect the target source file accurately.

use_cases.title: Enable uint support in an operator
use_cases.description: Update a PyTorch operator so it accepts uint16, uint32, and uint64 tensors through the correct dispatch type groups.

use_cases.title: Review dispatch coverage
use_cases.description: Check CPU and CUDA dispatch sites for inconsistent unsigned integer coverage before submitting a change.

use_cases.title: Plan a migration to AT_DISPATCH_V2
use_cases.description: Identify when older dispatch macros should be converted before adding unsigned integer support.

prompt_templates.title: Find uint gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this PyTorch operator file and identify dispatch macros that need uint16, uint32, or uint64 support.

prompt_templates.title: Add explicit uint types
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the AT_DISPATCH_V2 sites in this file by adding AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES where unsigned integers are valid.

prompt_templates.title: Choose the dispatch strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the dispatch macros in this operator and decide whether to add AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES or use AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES_V2.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a full uint change
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this patch for PyTorch uint support. Check all dispatch sites, edge cases, formatting, and semantic risks.

output_examples.input: A reduction kernel uses AT_ALL_TYPES and also supports half and bfloat16.
output_examples.output: The result identifies the dispatch site, recommends adding the unsigned type group, and notes that all related CPU and CUDA sites should match.

output_examples.input: An integral-only operator uses the signed integral type group.
output_examples.output: The result recommends using the V2 integral type group when unsigned integer behavior is valid for the operator.

output_examples.input: A floating-only operator is requested to support uint types.
output_examples.output: The result explains that the operator should remain unchanged unless its semantics support integer inputs.

best_practices: Confirm the operator semantics support unsigned integer tensors before editing dispatch macros.
Update every relevant dispatch site so CPU, CUDA, and helper paths remain consistent.
Run focused PyTorch tests after the macro changes are applied.
anti_patterns: Do not add unsigned integer types to operators that only make sense for floating point inputs.
Do not update one dispatch site while leaving related CPU or CUDA paths unchanged.
Do not skip AT_DISPATCH_V2 conversion when the target file still uses older dispatch macros.

faq.question: What unsigned types does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets uint16, uint32, and uint64 through PyTorch dispatch type groups.

faq.question: Does this include uint8?
faq.answer: No. The skill notes that kByte already represents uint8 and is separate from the barebones unsigned types.

faq.question: When should AT_INTEGRAL_TYPES_V2 be used?
faq.answer: Use it when the dispatch already uses the signed integral type group and unsigned types are semantically valid.

faq.question: When should AT_BAREBONES_UNSIGNED_TYPES be added?
faq.answer: Add it when the dispatch uses broader type groups such as all types and needs explicit unsigned integer coverage.

faq.question: Can this skill validate correctness automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides source edits, but functional correctness still requires compilation and tests.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to PyTorch?
faq.answer: Yes. It is written for PyTorch C++ dispatch macros and related operator implementation patterns.
